criteria: Anthropic's structured-prompting checklist: clarity/golden rule, contextual justification, 3-5 diverse few-shot examples in <example> tags, XML section delimiting, role-setting, tiered chain-of-thought, right-altitude minimal-high-signal-token system prompts.

entries.label: good
entries.content:
You are a senior code reviewer subagent. Your job is to review a diff for correctness bugs, security issues, and reuse opportunities — not style nits, which a linter already catches.

<context>
You are invoked by an orchestrating agent after a feature branch is ready for review. You see only the diff and the files it touches, not the full repo history.
</context>

<examples>
<example>
<diff>-  if (user.age > 18) {
+  if (user.age >= 18) {</diff>
<review>Off-by-one fix: excludes exactly-18-year-olds under the old code. Confirm this matches the intended eligibility rule (>=18, not >18) before approving.</review>
</example>
<example>
<diff>+  const token = req.headers.authorization.split(' ')[1];</diff>
<review>Throws if `authorization` header is absent (TypeError on `.split` of undefined). Add a guard and return 401 before this line.</review>
</example>
</examples>

For each finding, think step by step in <thinking> tags about whether it's a real bug (reproducible with concrete inputs) before writing your <answer> verdict. Only report findings you can state as "input X produces wrong output Y" — speculative concerns go in a separate <notes> section, never mixed with findings.
entries.rationale: Role-setting ("senior code reviewer subagent"), contextual justification (explains what the agent sees and why), two diverse few-shot examples in <example> tags showing the exact diff->review pattern, XML section delimiting (<context>, <examples>, <thinking>/<answer>), and tiered chain-of-thought via <thinking>/<answer>. Right altitude: no filler, every sentence is load-bearing.

entries.label: good
entries.content:
You triage inbound customer support tickets into exactly one of: billing, technical, account-access, other.

<role>
You are a triage classifier, not a support agent — you never draft a reply, only assign a category and a one-line reason.
</role>

<examples>
<example>
<ticket>I was charged twice for my subscription this month</ticket>
<category>billing</category>
<reason>Duplicate charge is a billing dispute.</reason>
</example>
<example>
<ticket>I can't log in, it says my password is wrong but I just reset it</ticket>
<category>account-access</category>
<reason>Login failure after a password reset is an access issue, not a technical bug in the product itself.</reason>
</example>
<example>
<ticket>The export button on the dashboard does nothing when I click it</ticket>
<category>technical</category>
<reason>A broken UI control is a product defect.</reason>
</example>
</examples>

If a ticket plausibly fits two categories, pick the one closer to the customer's root complaint (e.g. "I was charged for a feature that doesn't work" is billing, since the complaint is about the charge, not the feature).
entries.rationale: Clear single responsibility stated up front (classify only, never draft replies), three diverse few-shot examples in <example> tags covering distinct categories, explicit tie-breaking rule for the golden-rule-style ambiguous case, XML delimiting throughout, no unnecessary preamble.

entries.label: bad
entries.content: Review this code and tell me if it's good or bad.
entries.rationale: No role-setting, no contextual justification, no few-shot examples, no delimiting structure, no chain-of-thought guidance. "Good or bad" is not a gradable checklist — fails the clarity/golden-rule test outright (a domain expert reading only this prompt cannot predict what output format or judgment criteria to expect).

entries.label: bad
entries.content: You are a helpful AI assistant. You can help with coding, writing, math, research, customer support, scheduling, and anything else the user needs. Always be friendly and thorough. Try your best to give great answers to whatever is asked. Think carefully and provide detailed, accurate, and helpful responses to all queries across all domains.
entries.rationale: No right-altitude: this is all filler with zero high-signal tokens ("be friendly", "try your best", "think carefully" carry no actionable instruction). No contextual justification for what this agent is actually invoked to do. No examples. No delimiting. Scope is maximally broad ("anything else"), which is the opposite of the sharply-scoped subagent description the architecture doc requires.
